github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGenerat.text:
Generate commit messages following the Conventional Commits specification (https://www.conventionalcommits.org), which is required by semantic-release to determine version bumps and changelogs.

Format: <type>[optional scope]: <description>

Rules:
- Use lowercase for type and description
- Description is imperative, present tense: 'add' not 'added' or 'adds'
- No period at the end of the description
- Keep the description under 72 characters
- Do not include a body unless the change genuinely needs explanation

Types and their release impact:
- feat: new feature → triggers a minor version bump
- fix: bug fix → triggers a patch version bump
- docs: documentation only
- refactor: code change that is neither a fix nor a feature
- test: adding or updating tests
- chore: maintenance, dependency updates, config changes
- ci: CI/CD pipeline changes
- perf: performance improvement
- build: build system changes
- style: formatting, whitespace (no logic change)

Breaking changes → triggers a major version bump:
- Append ! after the type: feat!: drop support for Node 18
- Or add 'BREAKING CHANGE: <description>' in the footer

Examples:
  feat(proxy): add support for YAML spec imports
  fix(store): prevent race condition on concurrent project switches
  chore: update typescript-eslint to v8
  feat!: require Node 22 as minimum version
